title: clerodendrum cephalanthum subsp. cephalanthum
intro: Clerodendrum cephalanthum subsp. cephalanthum, also known as the "Fragrant Glory Bower", is a plant species in the Lamiaceae family, known for its vibrant purple flowers and fragrant foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been found to aid in the treatment of various health conditions. The health benefits of Clerodendrum cephalanthum subsp. cephalanthum include relief from fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
